description_long: This object is a compact, medieval-style crossbow with a wooden stock and a black, curved bow. The central body is made of textured wood, featuring a rectangular section where the bow is mounted and a metal trigger mechanism beneath. The bow itself is a single, slightly arched piece, attached to the stock with visible fastenings and a taut string running from tip to tip. The rear of the stock is squared off, while the front features a simple, open sight. The handle is integrated into the wooden body, and the overall design is utilitarian, with no visible cockpit, windows, or modern helicopter features. The object appears robust and functional, suitable for handheld use.
description: A compact, medieval-style crossbow with a wooden stock and a black, curved bow, featuring a taut string and metal trigger mechanism.
description_view_0: Front view showing the curved black bow and taut string, with the wooden stock and handle visible behind.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the wooden stock, the metal trigger mechanism, and the attachment of the bow.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the wooden stock and the full span of the black bow.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the bow's attachment and the trigger mechanism beneath the stock.
